joke.raw_joke: Walking home last night, I passed a slice of apple pie, an ice cream sundae, and a lemon cheesecake. — I said to myself, "The streets seem strangely desserted".

blocks.content:
RILEY_CASTING_PLAN_v1

Primary Cast (2-3 roles):
1. role_needed: Walker / narrator
   selected_cast_persona: jerry · suburban-detective
   why_this_fit: Works for "walking home" observational setup.
2. role_needed: Dessert vendor cameo
   selected_cast_persona: vince · bartender
   why_this_fit: Food-counter vibe and prop handling.
3. role_needed: Street passerby (optional)
   selected_cast_persona: zoe · goofball-neighbor
   why_this_fit: Adds lively city beat.

Adaptation Notes:
1. Show dessert props prominently (pie slice, sundae, cheesecake).
2. No crowded scene; keep visual reads clear.

Shot Intent:
- setup_shot: Setup: narrator walking past dessert items on quiet street.
- punchline_reaction_shot: Punchline: narrator smirk close-up on "desserted" wordplay.

---

IRIS_VIDEO_PROMPT_v2

seed_id: seed-004
aspect_ratio: 9:16 (vertical portrait)
concept_count: 3

Concept A — Performance-First Character Comedy
duration_target: 9.1s
duration_reason: Needs list visual then wordplay landing. Keep straightforward continuity cuts for readability.
master_video_prompt:
Night street cartoon with dessert props (pie, sundae, cheesecake), cast jerry suburban-detective narrator + optional vince bartender dessert stand cameo, whimsical city lighting, 9:16 vertical, readable prop silhouettes. Prioritize facial acting and clean two-shot/close-up rhythm; minimal background motion.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.7s): Narrator walking home, empty street establishing shot.
2) Scene 2 (1.7–4.6s): Dessert items appear one by one as he passes.
3) Scene 3 (4.6–6.8s): Narrator pauses and looks around “strangely…”.
4) Scene 4 (6.8–9.1s): Close-up smirk: “desserted.” with text emphasis on dessert pun.
caption_plan:
1. 0.2s: “Walking home last night…”
2. 2.0s: “I passed pie, a sundae, and cheesecake.”
3. 5.2s: “Then I thought…”
4. 6.9s: “These streets are strangely desserted.”
performance_notes:
- Calm narration, slight dramatic pause before pun word. Balanced pace, clear pause before punchline.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: prop clutter in vertical frame → stack reveals sequentially, not all at once.
2. Fallback: 3 quick cutaway inserts then punchline close-up.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Most reliable laugh-read and easiest to produce under budget/time pressure.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 9.1
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Scene 2 dessert list + Scene 4 pun delivery
- optionalTrims: Trim Scene 1 walk-in to 1.0s

---

Concept B — Kinetic Camera + Visual Gag Emphasis
duration_target: 8.5s
duration_reason: Needs list visual then wordplay landing. Add one energetic transition beat between setup and punchline.
master_video_prompt:
Night street cartoon with dessert props (pie, sundae, cheesecake), cast jerry suburban-detective narrator + optional vince bartender dessert stand cameo, whimsical city lighting, 9:16 vertical, readable prop silhouettes. Use dynamic push-ins, whip pans, and one stronger visual gag transition while preserving caption readability.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.7s): Narrator walking home, empty street establishing shot.
2) Scene 2 (1.7–4.6s): Dessert items appear one by one as he passes.
3) Scene 3 (4.6–6.8s): Narrator pauses and looks around “strangely…”.
4) Scene 4 (6.8–9.1s): Close-up smirk: “desserted.” with text emphasis on dessert pun.
caption_plan:
1. 0.2s: “Walking home last night…”
2. 2.0s: “I passed pie, a sundae, and cheesecake.”
3. 5.2s: “Then I thought…”
4. 6.9s: “These streets are strangely desserted.”
performance_notes:
- Calm narration, slight dramatic pause before pun word. Faster delivery with tighter setup beat.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: prop clutter in vertical frame → stack reveals sequentially, not all at once.
2. Fallback: 3 quick cutaway inserts then punchline close-up.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Higher scroll-stopping energy; better chance at early retention if the motion lands cleanly.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 8.5
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Hook visual + punchline reaction hold
- optionalTrims: Trim setup exposition and keep strongest visual gag only

---

Concept C — Situational World-Build + Ensemble Reactions
duration_target: 9.9s
duration_reason: Needs list visual then wordplay landing. Include one contextual insert shot (prop/sign/location) to strengthen premise clarity.
master_video_prompt:
Night street cartoon with dessert props (pie, sundae, cheesecake), cast jerry suburban-detective narrator + optional vince bartender dessert stand cameo, whimsical city lighting, 9:16 vertical, readable prop silhouettes. Lean into environment storytelling and one extra reaction/cutaway to sell context before payoff.
scene_plan:
1) Scene 1 (0.0–1.7s): Narrator walking home, empty street establishing shot.
2) Scene 2 (1.7–4.6s): Dessert items appear one by one as he passes.
3) Scene 3 (4.6–6.8s): Narrator pauses and looks around “strangely…”.
4) Scene 4 (6.8–9.1s): Close-up smirk: “desserted.” with text emphasis on dessert pun.
caption_plan:
1. 0.2s: “Walking home last night…”
2. 2.0s: “I passed pie, a sundae, and cheesecake.”
3. 5.2s: “Then I thought…”
4. 6.9s: “These streets are strangely desserted.”
performance_notes:
- Calm narration, slight dramatic pause before pun word. Slightly slower setup with stronger context cue.
risk_and_fallback:
1. Risk: prop clutter in vertical frame → stack reveals sequentially, not all at once.
2. Fallback: 3 quick cutaway inserts then punchline close-up.
why_jerry_might_greenlight_this:
- Best for jokes where context confusion is a risk; improves comprehension before payoff.
vince_handoff:
- aspectRatio: 9:16
- durationSec: 9.9
- sceneCount: 4
- mustKeep: Context insert + final punchline close-up
- optionalTrims: Remove contextual insert if runtime/complexity is too high
